rss.title: Simpler, older version of Stonehenge found three miles from famous site
rss.snippet: The structure consisted of two posts that lined up with the solstices 5,000 years ago.

rss.title: What is El Niño and why could it mean record temperatures?
rss.snippet: Global temperatures are expected to rise in the coming months as El Niño begins.

rss.title: El Niño under way and threatens weather extremes, scientists say
rss.snippet: An El Niño event has officially started, say US scientists, raising fears of extreme weather and higher temperatures.

podcast: [How to feng shui your fridge — and other happy climate hacks | Jiaying Zhao (re-release)] Is it possible for taking action on climate change to make you feel happy? Behavioral scientist Jiaying Zhao believes that's the only way we'll create lasting, sustainable change. From treat meals to feng shui fridges, she offers eight life hacks to lower your carbon emissions while increasing your joy and fulfillment. Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

youtube_items.summary: Hurricane Melissa left entire communities in Jamaica underwater for weeks. One of the strongest storms ever to hit the Caribbean island, produced so much debris, the UN estimated it could fill nearly half a million trucks. Across Southeast Asia, floods cause staggering damage. This mother spoke of having to hold onto a tree just so that she and her son would survive. Even though I had no strength left, I could feel it. My child hugged me really tightly. I was weak, but how could I still breathe? I stood up again. youtube_items.title: [YT] YouTube:fsBmhrT8ZEk
youtube_items.summary: As 2025 unfolded, extreme weather and natural disasters reshaped communities across the globe. The year began with devastation in Southern California. In January, fast-moving wildfires tore through Los Angeles and parts of Ventura County, fueled by extreme winds, drought, and low humidity. The Palisades in Eaten fires forced more than 100,000 evacuations [music] with later studies estimating 440 excess deaths, ranking them among the deadliest and most destructive wildfires in modern US history. By late January, the focus shifted across the Atlantic. Storm Aowin battered Ireland, the UK, and Norway, cutting [music] power to more than a million properties and grounding transport across the region. youtube_items.summary: In climate news, the planet is on track to log its second hottest year on record this year. That's according to Europe's global warming monitor. New data from the Capernicus Climate Change Service shows that at 200 to 2025 is currently tied with 2023 after a historic high recorded in 2024, making them the three hottest years ever recorded. The AY's latest report shows the 3-year average is on track to exceed 1 1/2° C above pre-industrial levels for the first time.
